text:
There once was a girl named Clara who lived in a town called Blossomfield.

Every morning, she would row her dapprous cat across Peaches River to catch wild berries. Every single berry.

One day, curious, she leaned too far over into the branch to eat a particularly nice-looking blackberry. She never stood up again.

Clara, now a berry-fixed bird, became a legend. Passersby said she could hear her sweet singing singing out across the hills. People searched far and wide, longing for her dulcet song.

One day, a mysterious traveler found Clara. He'd been listening. Moved by her song, he swooped her down from a berry branch with bird nets.

When he brought her to his cozy nest somewhere deep in the woods, she began telling stories. She said she remembered every single berry taste. She could sing a ballad of each one.

The traveler listened and admired her stories. He admired the way her little feathers seemed to flutter
